title: requests — subscribed events
description: Cross-module reactions handled by this module. Most of the module's PUBLIC events fire from these listeners — they subscribe to workflow/approvals lifecycle events, filter on `action_key='access.grants.create'`, and re-broadcast as the matching AccessRequest event. This is the SHIM layer that decouples this module's public interface from workflow/approvals' internals.

listeners.purpose: Filters ApprovalRequested events to `action_key='access.grants.create'`. Fetches the requester + resolved approvers via the AccessRequestPresenter and re-broadcasts as `AccessRequestSubmitted`. This is the entry-point of the bridge — without this listener no downstream consumer of AccessRequest events would ever fire on submit.

Filters to `action_key='access.grants.create'`. Re-broadcasts as `AccessRequestApproved`. Note: this fires BEFORE the grant is materialised — the requester's approval notification includes optimistic language ('you should now be able to access X'). AccessRequestGrantMaterializer fires next on ApprovalExecuted.

The BRIDGE. Filters ApprovalExecuted to `action_key='access.grants.create'`. Calls `access/grants::GrantIssuer::issue()` with the request's requester as subject, requested resource as resource, and requested permissions as the grant scope. Writes back `context_json.materialised_grant_id = <new_grant_id>` on the approval_instance via `workflow/approvals::AppendInstanceContextAction`. Fires `AccessRequestGrantIssued` on success. On failure (grant creation raises), records an audit event + fires no downstream event — the approval_instance is left in `approved` status but with `context_json.materialised_grant_id=null` so operators can retry via `access-requests:remediate-orphaned-approvals`.
Filters to `action_key='access.grants.create'`. Re-broadcasts as `AccessRequestRejected`. Extracts the rejecting decision's comment as the human-readable reason for the requester's notification.

When a grant sourced from an access request is revoked (via `access/grants::RevokeGrantAction`), this listener finds the underlying approval_instance via `source_reference` and writes an `access_grant_revoked` audit note to `context_json.audit_trail[]`. Does NOT cancel the approval_instance — the request WAS approved historically; the grant was later revoked, which is a separate lifecycle event.
Deletes pending access requests for the erased tenant. Cascade FK on approval_instances.tenant_id already handles the row delete; this listener writes the compliance-side audit trail entry.

When a User is deleted, auto-cancels their pending access requests (POST to workflow/approvals::withdraw with system actor) so the approvers' inbox doesn't carry stale entries. Sets `context_json.cancellation_reason='requester_deleted'`.

When a resource type's permission set changes (e.g. `invoices.export` was removed from the resource type's declared permissions), the registry entry is refreshed on the next boot. This listener also clears the `access-requests:requestable-registry` cache key so the change takes effect without a redeploy.

notes: Every shim listener CHECKS `event.approvalInstance.action_key === 'access.grants.create'` at the top of `handle()` and returns immediately when it doesn't match — keeping the shim cost effectively zero for the majority of approval instances that belong to other actions (refunds, billing changes, etc.).

Listener queue placement: SHIM listeners run on `notifications` because their sole side effect is to fire a downstream event that the notification pipeline reacts to. AccessRequestGrantMaterializer runs on the DEFAULT queue because it performs a DB write against access/grants and needs the normal-priority worker; separating it from the notification queue means a burst of decisions doesn't stall the grant-materialisation work.
There is NO listener on `workflow/approvals::ApprovalEscalated` — the escalation flow is deferred to Wave 2 per §10 of readme.md.
